episode_meta.title: Aliya Itzkowitz & Sam Gould (Part One): Insights from FT Strategies' AI Design Sprint Day

ai_tools: Virtual avatars (referenced from Philippines use case)

use_cases.description: Reformatting/repackaging content — turning articles into bullet points, shorter versions, different formats for different audiences
use_cases.organization: Multiple publishers (FT Strategies design sprint participants)
use_cases.outcome: Most popular use case category (~70% of ideas with sub-editing combined)

use_cases.description: Sub-editing and checking functions — applying style guides, checking for errors before publication
use_cases.outcome: Second most popular category (combined ~70% with reformatting)

use_cases.description: Email routing — automatically routing inbound news tips/emails to correct departments
Text-to-video — creating short-form video from written stories to target younger readers
Automating sheep auction reporting section of local newspaper using custom model

key_claims.claim: About 70% of AI use case ideas from publishers fell into two categories: content reformatting/personalization and sub-editing/checking functions
key_claims.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

key_claims.claim: The news industry tends to approach new technologies from a position of loss aversion rather than opportunity
key_claims.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

key_claims.claim: Generative AI has made AI more accessible — no longer need expensive data labeling and heavy infrastructure to get started
key_claims.speaker: Sam Gould

key_claims.claim: Western publishers focus more on intellectual property concerns while Asian publishers are more concerned about fake news/misinformation
key_claims.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

key_claims.claim: When top publications block ChatGPT from crawling their sites, it degrades the quality of information in public AI models
key_claims.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

challenges.description: Publishers paralyzed by the breadth of AI possibilities — too many options leads to inaction
Getting buy-in from rest of organization and securing funding; demonstrating ROI
Buy vs. build decision — whether to invest in building custom solutions or purchase existing tools
Information quality concerns — AI-generated misinformation, hallucinations, especially heading into election year
Job loss fears among newsroom employees
Digital divide: structural limitations (infrastructure, political challenges) in Global South limit AI adoption
Top publications blocking AI crawlers degrades quality of public AI models, creating information inequality

quotes.text: As an industry, we've really been talking about AI for most of twenty twenty-three. But then it's only a few publishers that are really beginning to leverage it in a meaningful way and actually implement AI-driven solutions.
quotes.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

quotes.text: We were internally calling this day a non-technical hackathon because we weren't coding up this idea. But we were thinking about it more in terms of how do we mobilize the organization around this opportunity.
quotes.speaker: Sam Gould

quotes.text: Someone had written 'garbage tsunami' at the beginning of the day. And then at the end of the day, they wrote 'opportunity tsunami.'
quotes.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

quotes.text: I'm concerned that [building in-house models] might create division between haves and have nots. And I'm particularly concerned because... when a bunch of top publications ban ChatGPT from crawling their websites... what's feeding those public models [is] probably lower quality information.
quotes.speaker: Aliya Itzkowitz

quotes.text: Generative AI is more accessible typically than AI of the past, in that you don't need an expensive process to go and label and clean thousands and thousands of data points and to spin up heavy infrastructure to train those models.
quotes.speaker: Sam Gould

recommendations.advice: Start with identifying business challenges/opportunities first, then see which ones AI can solve — don't start with the technology
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould

recommendations.advice: Validate user needs before making large investments in AI tools — run small proof of concepts first
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould

recommendations.advice: Bring cross-functional, diverse teams together when designing AI solutions — this leads to more robust, safer solutions
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould

recommendations.advice: Create clear action plans with named accountability — assign specific people to own AI initiatives
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould / Aliya Itzkowitz

recommendations.advice: Bring responsible AI into the conversation from the very beginning of the design process
recommendations.speaker: Sam Gould

external_references: Google News Initiative (GNI) — partnership for AI design sprint
Journalism AI (Charlie Beckett, LSE) — global report on AI in journalism
FT Strategies AI Design Sprint methodology
EU AI Act (mentioned in context of European regulatory approach)
Data Miner (Aliya's former employer, AI for newsrooms)
